pages.full_text:
have been part of the forum. Unfortunately the Amphitheatre was closed because they were removing sets from Verdi's 'Nambucco', so we couldn't go inside.
	We checked emails at a cybercafe. Apparently William is back from S. America because he wanted to start his dissertation & he nearly got mugged by
pages.notes: Text begins mid-sentence, continuing from previous page. Postcard glued in: aerial photo of Nîmes amphitheatre with printed caption 'Nîmes' and 'vue aérienne des arènes'. 'Nambucco' is likely 'Nabucco' (Verdi opera) — transcribed as written. No handwritten page number visible on this page.

pages.full_text:
a man with a gun. The boys played on computer games. There was a fat computer nerd in charge of the cafe. Simon & Eve walked around. Eve bought a skirt. We all went to the Jardin de la Fontaine, which was rich in trees. It didn't have a fountain, but a bubbling spring, carried in from the hills.
				Marble Streets. I like the city.

Pont du Gard. Roman aqueduct built 2000 years ago by Agrippa, to take water from Uzès to Nîmes. 48.77 m high.
photo_captions: le Pont du Gard — monument romain
pages.notes: Handwritten page number 70 in top-right corner. Text at top continues from left page (mid-sentence). 'Marble Streets. I like the city.' appears to be written in slightly smaller/different hand, possibly added later as annotation. Postcard glued in: photo of Pont du Gard with printed caption 'le Pont du Gard' and 'monument romain'. Caption text beside/below postcard appears to be handwritten (not printed): 'Pont du Gard. Roman aqueduct built 2000 years ago by Agrippa, to take water from Uzès to Nîmes. 48.77 m high.' The spread appears to be from a France trip visiting Nîmes and the Pont du Gard; no year explicit on this page but volume covers 1989–2003.
